text:
Once upon a time, in the heart of a sleepy countryside town, there lived a cat named Felix. Felix was uniquely peculiar; not only did he purr in thirteen different pitches, but he also had the extraordinary ability to communicate in human words. One bright morning, as golden sunlight filtered through the old oak tree outside Mrs. Whiskers' window, Felix meowed softly.

"Oh dear," Mrs. Whiskers whispered to herself. "What did Felix say?"

Felix stretched out his tiny tail and meowed again.

"Did it say 'coffee?'"

Mrs. Whiskers fetched her cup of aromatic coffee. Back she went, grinning.

"No," Felix said, "it said 'breakfast.' I asked him what he wanted for breakfast."

She laughed and sat down, opening the window. Felix caught a flying insect and pranced over to her.

"Eating insects, Felix? But aren't they insects?"
